writable-id-registers: RULED 2026-07-17 (task 122 spike executor): genuinely absent on stock Ubuntu 6.8.0-134 — the per-register probe shows every enumerated ID_AA64* register writable (reduced write + read-back) or carrying no reducible field, EXCEPT ID_AA64PFR1_EL1, which is FROZEN (has reducible fields, accepts no changed write; its writable mask landed in later mainline than 6.8). Not an AA-0 stop condition (the stop is KVM/EL2, usable PMUv3, BR_RETIRED — all present). Consequence recorded: the AA-6(a) whole-surface synthetic ID-register freeze cannot fully install on THIS stock kernel. AA-6 runs on the AA-3 patched host kernel (linux-6.18.35); this row MUST be re-probed there and found present before AA-6(a) relies on it. If PFR1 is still frozen on the patched host, it becomes a named row in the AA-6 enforcement truth table (frozen-by-kernel, with HCR_EL2.TID3 trap-emulation evaluated as the enforcement level instead of a KVM-installed freeze).
